title: Applying Scaling Laws
concept: Scaling laws guide training budgets, letting teams predict a model's final performance before starting expensive training runs. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Compute Budgets: FLOPs.'

exercises.prompt: What is the main advantage of using scaling laws in AI training?

options.text: Predicting final performance before spending money
Making the model physically smaller to fit on phones
Generating funny images of scales and balances

exercises.explain: Scaling laws let teams calculate and predict performance beforehand, avoiding costly trials and errors.

cards.title: No More Guesswork
cards.body: Training a modern AI model can cost millions of dollars and take months. How do AI labs know their model will actually be smart enough before they press the 'Start' button? They use mathematical crystal balls called Scaling Laws!

cards.title: What are Scaling Laws?
cards.body: Scaling laws are mathematical formulas that describe how an AI's performance improves as you increase three key resources: computing power, data size, and the number of parameters (the model's brain cells). By looking at these laws, we can predict a model's final capability before we even start training it.

cards.title: The Wind Tunnel Test
cards.body: Imagine building a giant bridge. Instead of building the real, massive bridge immediately to see if it holds, engineers build small-scale models in a wind tunnel first. Scaling laws are like those wind tunnel tests: they let us run small, cheap experiments to know exactly how strong our giant AI model will be before we spend millions of dollars building it.

cards.title: Scaling in Action
cards.body: Before training a new state-of-the-art chatbot, a team trains 10 tiny versions of the model using different amounts of data and compute. They plot these results on a chart, draw a line, and follow it to see exactly how much data and computer power they need to beat the world's best model.

cards.title: Myth: We Just Guess and Hope
cards.body: You might think we just build the biggest model possible and hope for the best. In reality, blind guessing is too expensive. Teams spend weeks calculating exact scaling trajectories to ensure they don't waste millions of dollars on a model that is too small or lacks enough data.

cards.title: Predictive vs. Blind Training
cards.body: See how planning with scaling laws compares to training blindly without them.

left.text: Predicts performance early, saves millions, and optimizes data and compute size.

right.text: High risk of wasting budget on a model that is either too small or trained on too little data.

exercises.prompt: Which three key resources do scaling laws link to AI performance?

options.text: Compute power, data size, and model parameters
The number of software engineers and coding speed
The physical size of the computer screen
The speed of the office internet connection

exercises.explain: Scaling laws show the relationship between performance, compute power, dataset size, and model parameters.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Scaling laws are just wild guesses and cannot help predict how well a model will perform.
exercises.explain: Scaling laws are highly reliable formulas used by AI researchers to plan budgets and avoid wasting compute power.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the statement about scaling laws.
exercises.sentence: Scaling laws help engineers [blank] a model's final performance before starting expensive training runs.
exercises.explain: Scaling laws allow us to forecast or predict how well a model will perform long before we pay for the full training run.

exercises.prompt: Which of these shows an AI assistant using scaling laws to plan a training budget?

options.text: AI: 'Based on our small-scale tests, increasing compute by 10x and data by 5x will reach our target accuracy.'
Human: 'Let's just run the training overnight and see if it looks smart tomorrow morning.'
Human: 'I think we should use a blue background for the training log dashboard.'

exercises.explain: Option A is generated by a smart AI planning tool using scaling laws to optimize resource allocation, whereas the others are random guesses.

exercises.prompt: Why do AI companies rely so heavily on scaling laws before launching massive training runs?

options.text: They guarantee that a model will never make factual mistakes.
They allow teams to predict final performance using small test runs.
They automatically write the code for the neural network.
They reduce the size of the dataset needed to zero.

exercises.explain: Scaling laws are the ultimate blueprint for AI training, giving teams the math needed to forecast performance before spending huge amounts on training runs! Next up: Compute Budgets: FLOPs.
